content.full_text:
State of California 
Memor ndum 
To 
OPINION REQUEST MEETING PARTICIPANTS 
Date 
August 25, 1978 
From 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
Jay 
Subject: 
Mark Lubow, treasurer for Californians for Brown (213)746-6870, 
has asked the following question: 
If a state official, who is an Article 3 filer, travels 
with the Governor on campaign related activities and the 
official's travel expenses of $250 or more are paid by 
the campaign committee, does the official have a reporting 
obligation under Chapter 77 
I said that Lee would call him back on Tuesday.

sections.question: If a state official, who is an Article 3 filer, travels with the Governor on campaign related activities and the official's travel expenses of $250 or more are paid by the campaign committee, does the official have a reporting obligation under Chapter 7?
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a state Article 3 filer must report travel expenses of $250 or more paid by a campaign committee for Governor-related campaign activities.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: No conclusion or final answer provided in this document. This appears to be an internal FPPC memorandum documenting an incoming opinion request rather than a completed advice letter with FPPC guidance. The notation 'I said that Lee would call him back on Tuesday' suggests this is a case management note indicating the matter was pending response.
